riskFactors: USDY is an unregistered offshore restricted offering and has not been registered under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933 or securities/financial instrument laws of other jurisdictions.
USDY is issued by Ondo Global Markets (BVI) Limited, a British Virgin Islands business company that is not registered as an investment company, AIF, UCITS, or equivalent regulated fund vehicle.
Holders receive tokenized note exposure and do not have direct rights to hold or receive U.S. Treasuries, creating legal-structure and issuer dependency risk.
Reserve backing is mixed and may depend on issuance date, including short-term U.S. Treasuries, iShares Short Treasury Bond ETF shares, or bank demand deposits.
Proof-of-reserves is off-chain collateral reporting reviewed by Ankura Trust Company; no on-chain PoR oracle address or PoR chain is identified.
ReserveBreakdown, collateralizationRatio, auditor, lastAuditDate, and lastAuditUrl are not provided, limiting institutional reserve auditability.
USDC redemption is conditional on stablecoin swapper liquidity, and on-chain liquidity is materially smaller than total asset value.
Blocked jurisdictions include the United States and multiple sanctioned or restricted regions, limiting distribution and secondary-market participation.
The asset is deployed across many chains, increasing operational complexity and cross-chain monitoring requirements.
mitigants: USDY has large market presence with approximately 2.14B USD market cap/AUM and 14,554 holders in the current normalized dataset.
Official documentation describes USDY as backed by short-term U.S. Treasury-related collateral, SHV ETF shares, or bank demand deposits depending on issuance date.
Daily collateral reporting reviewed by Ankura Trust Company provides off-chain reserve oversight, although it is not equivalent to an on-chain proof-of-reserves oracle.
KYC onboarding, sanctions screening, and jurisdiction restrictions reduce regulatory distribution risk compared with unrestricted retail tokens.
Instant redemption to USDon and conditional USDC redemption provide a stronger issuer redemption path than assets relying only on secondary-market liquidity.
USDY has multi-chain availability across Ethereum, Mantle, Solana, Sui, Aptos, Noble, Arbitrum, Stellar, Plume, and Sei, supporting broad access and redundancy.
The token is designed as an accumulating token whose redemption value increases as yield accrues, with a rebasing version, rUSDY, also available.
